machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane platform component (links secretin to platform; XcpP/GspC homolog)
machinery_genes.quote: The 11 T2SS "core" genes txcP to txcZ (PSPA7_1417 to PSPA7_1407) present in RGP69 are arranged unidirectionally and may constitute a single operon.
machinery_genes.note: txcP is the first of the 11 named core T2SS genes. Figure 6c names TxcP individually and gives 29.2% identity to its Xcp counterpart. Xcp-to-Gsp mapping (XcpP=GspC inner-membrane platform) per the apparatus brief; Figure 6a aligns the txc and xcp gene maps letter-for-letter (P,Q,R,S,T,U,V,W,X,Y,Z).

machinery_genes.role: outer-membrane secretin (forms the OM secretion pore; XcpQ/GspD homolog)
machinery_genes.quote: The 11 T2SS "core" genes txcP to txcZ (PSPA7_1417 to PSPA7_1407) present in RGP69 are arranged unidirectionally and may constitute a single operon.
machinery_genes.note: Secretin family. The paper's Xcp reference states 'the multimer of the secretin XcpQ forms a pore in the outer membrane'; TxcQ is the letter-for-letter Xcp homolog (Figure 6a) and is named individually in Figure 6c with 51.8% identity to XcpQ. Highest-confidence call: secretin (XcpQ=GspD).

machinery_genes.quote: The 11 T2SS "core" genes txcP to txcZ (PSPA7_1417 to PSPA7_1407) present in RGP69 are arranged unidirectionally and may constitute a single operon.
machinery_genes.note: TxcR named individually in Figure 6c (67.1% identity to XcpR, the highest in the set). XcpR=GspE secretion ATPase per the apparatus brief; the Xcp inner-membrane platform is given as XcpPRSYZ in the paper's introduction.

machinery_genes.quote: The 11 T2SS "core" genes txcP to txcZ (PSPA7_1417 to PSPA7_1407) present in RGP69 are arranged unidirectionally and may constitute a single operon.
machinery_genes.note: TxcS named individually in Figure 6c (63.2% identity to XcpS). XcpS=GspF integral IM platform per the apparatus brief; part of the XcpPRSYZ inner-membrane platform named in the paper.

machinery_genes.role: major pseudopilin (builds the pseudopilus piston; XcpT/GspG homolog)
machinery_genes.quote: In this phylogenetic tree, the txc major pseudopilin gene txcT is located in the same monophylogenetic group as xcpT, while hxcT is nested within a large cluster of betaproteobacteria
machinery_genes.note: Directly named 'the txc major pseudopilin gene txcT' in the Discussion. XcpT=GspG major pseudopilin. Figure 6c gives 71.8% identity to XcpT (highest individual-protein identity to Xcp).

machinery_genes.quote: The 11 T2SS "core" genes txcP to txcZ (PSPA7_1417 to PSPA7_1407) present in RGP69 are arranged unidirectionally and may constitute a single operon.
machinery_genes.note: TxcU named individually in Figure 6c (38.7% identity to XcpU). XcpU=GspH minor pseudopilin per the apparatus brief (Xcp minor pseudopilins XcpU-X = GspH-K).

machinery_genes.quote: The 11 T2SS "core" genes txcP to txcZ (PSPA7_1417 to PSPA7_1407) present in RGP69 are arranged unidirectionally and may constitute a single operon.
machinery_genes.note: TxcV named individually in Figure 6c (41.8% identity to XcpV). XcpV=GspI minor pseudopilin per the apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: The 11 T2SS "core" genes txcP to txcZ (PSPA7_1417 to PSPA7_1407) present in RGP69 are arranged unidirectionally and may constitute a single operon.
machinery_genes.note: TxcW named individually in Figure 6c (33.3% identity to XcpW). XcpW=GspJ minor pseudopilin per the apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: The 11 T2SS "core" genes txcP to txcZ (PSPA7_1417 to PSPA7_1407) present in RGP69 are arranged unidirectionally and may constitute a single operon.
machinery_genes.note: TxcX named individually in Figure 6c (35.3% identity to XcpX). XcpX=GspK minor pseudopilin per the apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane platform component (binds ATPase; XcpY/GspL homolog)
machinery_genes.quote: The 11 T2SS "core" genes txcP to txcZ (PSPA7_1417 to PSPA7_1407) present in RGP69 are arranged unidirectionally and may constitute a single operon.
machinery_genes.note: TxcY named individually in Figure 6c (32.1% identity to XcpY). XcpY=GspL IM platform (binds the ATPase) per the apparatus brief; part of the XcpPRSYZ inner-membrane platform named in the paper.

machinery_genes.quote: The 11 T2SS "core" genes txcP to txcZ (PSPA7_1417 to PSPA7_1407) present in RGP69 are arranged unidirectionally and may constitute a single operon.
machinery_genes.note: TxcZ named individually in Figure 6c (36.1% identity to XcpZ); terminal gene of the operon. XcpZ=GspM IM platform per the apparatus brief; part of the XcpPRSYZ inner-membrane platform named in the paper.

machinery_genes.role: prepilin peptidase (matures the Txc pseudopilins; shared with Xcp and Hxc T2SSs and the type IV pilus)
machinery_genes.quote: Because no prepilin peptidases are encoded by RGP69, the Txc pseudopilins probably use the general prepilin peptidase XcpA/PilD (PSPA7_5164), which is also used by the Xcp and Hxc pseudopilins.
machinery_genes.note: Encoded OUTSIDE the txcP-Z operon (PSPA7_5164, not in RGP69) but explicitly tied to the Txc apparatus as the peptidase that matures the Txc pseudopilins. XcpA/PilD = GspO prepilin peptidase, shared across all three P. aeruginosa T2SSs and the type IV pilus.

notes: Txc = 'third Xcp homolog', the third complete T2SS of P. aeruginosa, found uniquely in strain PA7 and encoded by the RGP69 genome-plasticity region. Cadoret, Ball, Douzi & Voulhoux 2014 (J Bacteriol) is the sole functional characterization paper and is the apparatus source. The 11 core T2SS genes are named txcP to txcZ (PSPA7_1417 to PSPA7_1407) as a single likely operon; the corpus label 'Txc (txcQ-Z)' omits txcP, but the literature establishes the operon as txcP-Z (11 genes), so TxcP is included. Each of the 11 proteins (TxcP, TxcQ, TxcR, TxcS, TxcT, TxcU, TxcV, TxcW, TxcX, TxcY, TxcZ) is named individually in Figure 6c with its percent identity to the Xcp and Hxc counterparts, and Figure 6a aligns the txc and xcp gene maps letter-for-letter. Family/role assignments follow the Xcp-to-Gsp correspondence in the apparatus brief (XcpQ=GspD secretin, XcpR=GspE ATPase, XcpP/S/Y/Z = GspC/F/L/M inner-membrane platform [the paper names the Xcp platform as XcpPRSYZ], XcpT=GspG major pseudopilin [directly named 'txc major pseudopilin gene txcT'], XcpU/V/W/X = GspH/I/J/K minor pseudopilins). Only the secretin (TxcQ) and the major pseudopilin (TxcT) receive an explicit per-protein functional statement in the text; the remaining assignments rest on the unambiguous letter-for-letter Xcp homology shown in Figure 6 plus the operon-naming quote, which I treat as high confidence given the system's defining 'third Xcp homolog' identity. The prepilin peptidase XcpA/PilD (PSPA7_5164) is encoded outside RGP69 but is included as GspO because the paper states the Txc pseudopilins use it. Deliberately EXCLUDED as non-machinery: the secreted substrate CbpE (chitin-binding protein E, PSPA7-encoded in RGP69), the two-component regulatory system TtsS (sensor) / TtsR (response regulator), and the upstream Sec/Tat translocon. No GspN/GspS accessory genes are described for this operon.
